title: NCI60 Dataset — NCI60

title: NCI60 Dataset — NCI60

what: Trains a Random Forest of Project-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — PPForest
title: Trains a Random Forest of Project-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — PPForest
text: function trains Random Forest Project-Pursuit oblique decision tree (PPTree) using either formula data frame interface matrix-based interface. using formula interface, specify model formula data frame containing variables. matrix-based interface, provide matrices features labels directly. number trees controlled size parameter. tree trained stratified bootstrap sample drawn data. number variables consider split controlled n_vars parameter. lambda = 0, model trained using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA). lambda > 0, model trained using Penalized Discriminant Analysis (PDA).

title: Trains a Random Forest of Project-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — PPForest
code: PPForest(   formula = NULL,   data = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   size = 2,   lambda = 0,   n_vars = NULL,   seed = NULL,   n_threads = NULL )

title: Trains a Random Forest of Project-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — PPForest
text: formula formula form y ~ x1 + x2 + ..., y vector labels x1, x2, ... features. data data frame containing variables formula. x matrix containing features observation. y matrix containing labels observation. size number trees forest. lambda regularization parameter. lambda = 0, model trained using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA). lambda > 0, model trained using Penalized Discriminant Analysis (PDA). n_vars number variables consider split. chosen uniformly split. default variables. seed optional integer seed reproducibility. NULL (default), seed drawn R's RNG, set.seed() controls reproducibility. integer provided, value used directly. seed used training computing permuted variable importance. n_threads number threads use. default number cores available.

title: Trains a Random Forest of Project-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — PPForest
text: PPForest model trained x y.

title: Trains a Random Forest of Project-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — PPForest
code: # Example 1: formula interface with the `iris` dataset PPForest(Type ~ ., data = iris) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.01 -0.82 0.53 0.2 ] * x) < -0.6616913: #>   Predict: setosa  #> Else: #>  If ([ 0.09 0.48 -0.34 -0.8 ] * x) < -1.103081: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.34 0.63 -0.68 -0.15 ] * x) < 1.586351: #>  If ([ 0.15 0.38 -0.34 -0.85 ] * x) < -0.9924551: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #> Else: #>   Predict: setosa  #>  #>   # Example 2: formula interface with the `iris` dataset with regularization PPForest(Type ~ ., data = iris, lambda = 0.5) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.08 -0.72 0.43 0.53 ] * x) < 0.03756618: #>   Predict: setosa  #> Else: #>  If ([ 0.05 0.11 -0.31 -0.94 ] * x) < -2.512249: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.11 -0.63 0.55 0.53 ] * x) < 0.9280792: #>   Predict: setosa  #> Else: #>  If ([ 0.01 -0.22 0.36 0.91 ] * x) < 2.788582: #>    Predict: versicolor  #>  Else: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  #>   # Example 3: matrix interface with the `iris` dataset PPForest(x = iris[, 1:4], y = iris[, 5]) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.27 0.63 -0.69 -0.21 ] * x) < 1.063238: #>  If ([ 0.31 0.08 -0.48 -0.82 ] * x) < -1.619433: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #> Else: #>   Predict: setosa  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.23 0.72 -0.65 -0.03 ] * x) < 1.453875: #>  If ([ 0.21 0.49 -0.55 -0.64 ] * x) < -1.026834: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #> Else: #>   Predict: setosa  #>  #>   # Example 4: matrix interface with the `iris` dataset with regularization PPForest(x = iris[, 1:4], y = iris[, 5], lambda = 0.5) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.08 -0.67 0.43 0.6 ] * x) < 0.2777239: #>   Predict: setosa  #> Else: #>  If ([ 0.02 0.05 -0.24 -0.97 ] * x) < -2.578496: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.09 -0.68 0.46 0.56 ] * x) < 0.3777013: #>   Predict: setosa  #> Else: #>  If ([ 0.01 -0.15 0.33 0.93 ] * x) < 2.815652: #>    Predict: versicolor  #>  Else: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  #>   # Example 5: formula interface with the `crabs` dataset PPForest(Type ~ . - sex + as.numeric(as.factor(sex)), data = crabs) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.64 0.06 -0.05 -0.47 0.59 0.13 ] * x) < 0.6518859: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.65 0.13 0.13 -0.58 0.42 0.17 ] * x) < 1.011468: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>  #>   # Example 6: formula interface with the `crabs` dataset with regularization PPForest(Type ~ . - sex + as.numeric(as.factor(sex)), data = crabs, lambda = 0.5) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.39 0.18 0 -0.07 0.37 -0.82 ] * x) < 9.471808: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.55 0.12 0.01 -0.09 0.51 0.64 ] * x) < 15.25045: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>  #>   # Example 7: matrix interface with the `crabs` dataset x <- crabs[, 2:5] x$sex <- as.numeric(as.factor(crabs$sex)) PPForest(x = x, y = crabs$Type) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.64 0.24 0.22 -0.53 0.45 ] * x) < 1.742541: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.75 0.16 0.25 -0.56 0.19 ] * x) < 1.37413: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>  #>   # Example 8: matrix interface with the `crabs` dataset with regularization x <- crabs[, 2:5] x$sex <- as.numeric(as.factor(crabs$sex)) PPForest(x = x, y = crabs$Type, lambda = 0.5) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.82 0.41 0.07 -0.05 -0.39 ] * x) < 18.13947: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.91 0.35 0.06 -0.08 -0.2 ] * x) < 17.40269: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>  #>

what: Trains a Project-Pursuit oblique decision tree. — PPTree
title: Trains a Project-Pursuit oblique decision tree. — PPTree
text: function trains Project-Pursuit oblique decision tree (PPTree) using either formula data frame interface matrix-based interface. using formula interface, specify model formula data frame containing variables. matrix-based interface, provide matrices features labels directly. lambda = 0, model trained using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA). lambda > 0, model trained using Penalized Discriminant Analysis (PDA).

title: Trains a Project-Pursuit oblique decision tree. — PPTree
code: PPTree(   formula = NULL,   data = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   lambda = 0,   seed = NULL )

title: Trains a Project-Pursuit oblique decision tree. — PPTree
text: formula formula form y ~ x1 + x2 + ..., y vector labels x1, x2, ... features. data data frame containing variables formula. x matrix containing features observation. y matrix containing labels observation. lambda regularization parameter. lambda = 0, model trained using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA). lambda > 0, model trained using Penalized Discriminant Analysis (PDA). seed optional integer seed reproducibility. NULL (default), seed drawn R's RNG, set.seed() controls reproducibility. integer provided, value used directly.

title: Trains a Project-Pursuit oblique decision tree. — PPTree
text: PPTree model trained x y.

title: Trains a Project-Pursuit oblique decision tree. — PPTree
code: # Example 1: formula interface with the `iris` dataset PPTree(Type ~ ., data = iris) #>  #> Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree: #> If ([ 0.19 0.71 -0.66 -0.17 ] * x) < 1.070804: #>  If ([ 0.23 0.36 -0.44 -0.79 ] * x) < -1.062904: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #> Else: #>   Predict: setosa  #>   # Example 2: formula interface with the `iris` dataset with regularization PPTree(Type ~ ., data = iris, lambda = 0.5) #>  #> Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree: #> If ([ 0.07 -0.68 0.45 0.58 ] * x) < 0.2732373: #>   Predict: setosa  #> Else: #>  If ([ 0.01 0.16 -0.34 -0.93 ] * x) < -2.714733: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #>   # Example 3: matrix interface with the `iris` dataset PPTree(x = iris[, 1:4], y = iris[, 5]) #> If ([ 0.19 0.71 -0.66 -0.17 ] * x) < 1.070804: #>  If ([ 0.23 0.36 -0.44 -0.79 ] * x) < -1.062904: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #> Else: #>   Predict: setosa  #>   # Example 4: matrix interface with the `iris` dataset with regularization PPTree(x = iris[, 1:4], y = iris[, 5], lambda = 0.5) #> If ([ 0.07 -0.68 0.45 0.58 ] * x) < 0.2732373: #>   Predict: setosa  #> Else: #>  If ([ 0.01 0.16 -0.34 -0.93 ] * x) < -2.714733: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #>   # Example 5: formula interface with the `crabs` dataset PPTree(Type ~ . - sex + as.numeric(as.factor(sex)), data = crabs) #>  #> Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree: #> If ([ 0.59 0.17 0.09 -0.55 0.48 0.29 ] * x) < 1.136014: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>   # Example 6: formula interface with the `crabs` dataset with regularization PPTree(Type ~ . - sex + as.numeric(as.factor(sex)), data = crabs, lambda = 0.5) #>  #> Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree: #> If ([ 0.71 0.25 0 -0.13 0.65 -0.04 ] * x) < 18.34709: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>   # Example 7: matrix interface with the `crabs` dataset x <- crabs[, 2:5] x$sex <- as.numeric(as.factor(crabs$sex)) PPTree(x = x, y = crabs$Type) #> If ([ 0.67 0.16 0.31 -0.59 0.27 ] * x) < 1.40659: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>   # Example 8: matrix interface with the `crabs` dataset with regularization x <- crabs[, 2:5] x$sex <- as.numeric(as.factor(crabs$sex)) PPTree(x = x, y = crabs$Type, lambda = 0.5) #> If ([ 0.89 0.41 0.06 -0.08 0.17 ] * x) < 18.43002: #>   Predict: B  #> Else: #>   Predict: O  #>

title: Australian Crabs Dataset — crab
text: Measurements rock crabs genus Leptograpsus. dataset contains 200 observations  two species crab (blue orange), 50 specimens sex species,   collected site Fremantle, Western Australia. Type class variable 4 classes combinations specie sex (BlueMale, BlueFemale, OrangeMale OrangeFemale) FL size frontal lobe length, mm RW rear width, mm CL length midline carapace, mm CW maximum width carapace, mm BD depth body; females, measured displacement abdomen, mm

title: Australian Crabs Dataset — crab
code: data(crab)

title: Australian Crabs Dataset — crab
text: data frame 200 rows 6 variables

title: Australian Crabs Dataset — crab
text: Campbell, N. . & Mahon, R. J. (1974), Multivariate Study Variation Two Species Rock Crab genus Leptograpsus, Australian Journal Zoology 22(3), 417 - 425.

title: Crabs Dataset — crabs
text: Measurements 200 crabs genus Leptograpsus, 50 crabs two colour forms sexes. Measurements include frontal lobe size, rear width, carapace length, carapace width, body depth.

title: Crabs Dataset — crabs
text: data frame 200 rows 8 variables: Type Species code, `B` indicating blue `O` indicating orange. sex Sex crab, `M` indicating male `F` indicating female. FL Size frontal lobe mm. RW Rear width mm. CL Carapace length mm. CW Carapace width mm. BD Body depth mm.

title: Crabs Dataset — crabs
text: Campbell, N.., Mahon, R.J. (1974) multivariate study variation two species rock crab genus Leptograpsus. Australian Journal Zoology, 22, 417-425.

text: Contains measurements 214 observations 6 types glass; defined terms oxide content. Type 6 types glasses X1 refractive index X2 Sodium (unit measurement: weight percent corresponding oxide). X3 Magnesium X4 Aluminum X5 Silicon X6 Potassium X7 Calcium X8 Barium X9 Iron

title: Image Dataset — image
text: Contains  2310 observations instances 7 outdoor images Type 7 types outdoor images, brickface, cement,  foliage, grass, path, sky, window. X1 column center pixel region X2 row center pixel region. X3 number pixels region = 9. X4 results line extraction algorithm counts many lines length 5 (orientation) low contrast, less equal 5, go region. X5 measure contrast horizontally adjacent pixels region. 6, mean standard deviation given. attribute used vertical edge detector. X6 X5 sd X7 measures contrast vertically adjacent pixels. Used horizontal line detection. X8 sd X7 X9 average region (R + G + B)/3 X10 average region R value. X11 average region B value. X12 average region G value. X13 measure excess red: (2R - (G + B)) X14 measure excess blue: (2B - (G + R)) X15 measure excess green: (2G - (R + B)) X16 3-d nonlinear transformation RGB. (Algorithm can found Foley VanDam, Fundamentals Interactive Computer Graphics) X17 mean X16 X18 hue  mean

code: data(image)

text: data frame contains 2310 observations 19 variables

title: Iris Flower Dataset — iris
text: Iris flower dataset Fisher's Iris dataset multivariate dataset introduced Sir Ronald Fisher 1936 example discriminant analysis. includes measurements 150 iris flowers three species, 50 species. measurements include length width sepals petals.

title: Iris Flower Dataset — iris
text: data frame 150 rows 5 variables: Sepal.Length Length sepal cm. Sepal.Width Width sepal cm. Petal.Length Length petal cm. Petal.Width Width petal cm. Type factor levels `setosa`, `versicolor`, `virginica` indicating species observation.

title: Iris Flower Dataset — iris
text: Fisher, R.. (1936) use multiple measurements taxonomic problems. Annals Eugenics, 7, Part II, 179-188.

text: dataset comes study gene expression two types acute leukemias, acute lymphoblastic leukemia () acute myeloid leukemia (AML). Gene expression levels measured using Affymetrix high density oligonucleotide arrays containing 6817 human genes. dataset containing 72 observations 3 leukemia types classes. Type 3 classes 38 cases B-cell , 25 cases AML 9 cases T-cell Gene1 Gen 40 gene expression levels

title: Plot a PPForest model. — plot.PPForest
text: Visualizes PPForest model. default, shows variable importance across available metrics. Use type show individual plots.

title: Plot a PPForest model. — plot.PPForest
code: # S3 method for class 'PPForest' plot(x, type = "importance", tree_index = 1L, node = 1L, ...)

title: Plot a PPForest model. — plot.PPForest
text: x PPForest model. type Character string specifying plot type. "importance" (default) shows variable importance (available metrics), "structure" shows specific tree embedded histograms, "projection" shows projected data node, "boundaries" shows decision boundaries specific tree. tree_index Integer index tree plot (1-based). used type = "structure", type = "projection", type = "boundaries". Defaults 1. node Integer index node projection plots. Defaults 1 (root). used type = "projection". ... Additional arguments passed internal plotting function.

title: Plot a PPForest model. — plot.PPForest
text: ggplot2 object (invisibly).

title: Plot a PPForest model. — plot.PPForest
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ forest <- PPForest(Type ~ ., data = iris, size = 10) plot(forest) plot(forest, type = "structure", tree_index = 1) plot(forest, type = "projection", tree_index = 1) } # }

title: Plot a PPTree model. — plot.PPTree
text: Visualizes PPTree model. default, shows mosaic overview tree structure, decision boundaries, variable importance. Use type show individual plots.

title: Plot a PPTree model. — plot.PPTree
code: # S3 method for class 'PPTree' plot(x, type = NULL, node = 1L, ...)

title: Plot a PPTree model. — plot.PPTree
text: x PPTree model. type Character string specifying plot type. NULL (default) shows mosaic overview. options: "structure" tree embedded histograms, "importance" variable importance (available metrics), "projection" projected data node, "boundaries" decision boundaries feature space. node Integer index node projection plots (1-based, breadth-first order). Defaults 1 (root node). used type = "projection". ... Additional arguments passed internal plotting function.

title: Plot a PPTree model. — plot.PPTree
text: ggplot2 object (invisibly), NULL mosaic layout.

title: Plot a PPTree model. — plot.PPTree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ model <- PPTree(Type ~ ., data = iris) plot(model)                         # mosaic overview plot(model, type = "structure")     # tree structure only plot(model, type = "importance")    # variable importance plot(model, type = "projection")   # projection histogram plot(model, type = "boundaries")   # decision boundaries } # }

what: Parsnip model specification for PPForest. — pp_forest
title: Parsnip model specification for PPForest. — pp_forest
text: Creates model specification Projection Pursuit random forest. Use set_engine("PPTree") select PPTree engine.

title: Parsnip model specification for PPForest. — pp_forest
code: pp_forest(mode = "classification", trees = NULL, mtry = NULL, penalty = NULL)

title: Parsnip model specification for PPForest. — pp_forest
text: mode character string model type. "classification" supported. trees number trees forest (maps size). mtry number variables consider split (maps n_vars). penalty regularization parameter (maps lambda).

title: Parsnip model specification for PPForest. — pp_forest
text: parsnip model specification.

title: Parsnip model specification for PPForest. — pp_forest
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(parsnip) spec <- pp_forest(trees = 50, mtry = 2) %>% set_engine("PPTree") fit <- spec %>% fit(Type ~ ., data = iris) predict(fit, iris) predict(fit, iris, type = "prob") } # }

what: Parsnip model specification for PPTree. — pp_tree
title: Parsnip model specification for PPTree. — pp_tree
text: Creates model specification single Projection Pursuit decision tree. Use set_engine("PPTree") select PPTree engine.

title: Parsnip model specification for PPTree. — pp_tree
code: pp_tree(mode = "classification", penalty = NULL)

title: Parsnip model specification for PPTree. — pp_tree
text: mode character string model type. "classification" supported. penalty regularization parameter (maps lambda).

title: Parsnip model specification for PPTree. — pp_tree
text: parsnip model specification.

title: Parsnip model specification for PPTree. — pp_tree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(parsnip) spec <- pp_tree(penalty = 0) %>% set_engine("PPTree") fit <- spec %>% fit(Type ~ ., data = iris) predict(fit, iris) } # }

title: Prints a PPForest model. — print.PPForest
code: model <- PPForest(Type ~ ., data = iris) print(model) #>  #> Random Forest of Project-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #> ------------------------------------- #> Tree 1: #> If ([ 0.04 0.78 -0.53 -0.32 ] * x) < 0.6079259: #>  If ([ 0.05 0.31 -0.24 -0.92 ] * x) < -1.518452: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #> Else: #>   Predict: setosa  #>  #> Tree 2: #> If ([ 0.26 0.68 -0.67 -0.16 ] * x) < 1.333983: #>  If ([ 0.23 0.41 -0.34 -0.81 ] * x) < -0.4588342: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor  #> Else: #>   Predict: setosa  #>  #>
